spec_match.rationale: The lab now visualizes the XRVisibilityMaskChangeEvent vertices and indices as a triangle mesh and shows how that payload feeds a render stencil/foveation budget.

interactive.rationale: Visitors can change headset mask profile, vertex detail, render target scale, event source, selected vertex, simulate events, probe immersive-vr support, and reset the lab while all readouts update live.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept lede now echoes the feature-index promise: visibilitymaskchange delivers viewport mesh vertices and indices so clipped pixels can be skipped.

edge_cases.rationale: The page exposes WebXR/event/handler feature detection, an unsupported fallback branch, and an immersive-vr probe rejection path for devices without a usable XR session.

references.rationale: The concept includes the required ChromeStatus link for feature 5073760055066624 and the WebXR XRVisibilityMaskChangeEvent spec reference.

design_system.rationale: Raw color literals and generated inline event handlers/styles were removed; the page uses design tokens, form controls, a canvas stage, readouts, and a standard references section.

summary: Mask Mesh Inspector is now an interactive XR visibility-mask mesh lab with vertex/index visualization, foveated render savings, feature detection, unsupported fallback behavior, complete references, and no remaining critique tasks.
